victim_side: Bosnian Muslims of the Srebrenica enclave
geographic_scope: Potočari UN compound and nearby factories/fields
excerpt: ICTY para. 37 places the Potočari crowd at 20,000-25,000 on the evening of 11 July 1995.
transform_note: Used as a civilian exposure input; likely includes a small number of men of unclear status, which may slightly overstate civilian person-months.
confidence_note: Moderate confidence in crowd size; lower confidence in exact status composition.

victim_side: Bosnian Muslim men fleeing toward Tuzla
geographic_scope: Jaglići/Šušnjari assembly point into the breakout corridor toward Tuzla
excerpt: ICTY para. 61 gives a 10,000-15,000 range for the retreating column.
transform_note: Used as the parent population later decomposed into soldiers and civilians for exposure calculations.
confidence_note: Moderate; witness-based range rather than a roster.

victim_side: Bosnian Muslim soldiers of the 28th Division within the breakout column
geographic_scope: Breakout column from Srebrenica toward Tuzla
excerpt: ICTY para. 61 states that about one third of the men in the column were soldiers from the 28th Division.
transform_note: Used to derive military and civilian person-month exposure from the mixed column population.
confidence_note: Useful operational rule, but still approximate.

victim_side: Bosniaks/Bosnian Muslims tied to the fall of Srebrenica
geographic_scope: Srebrenica-related municipalities in Podrinje tied to the fall of the UN Safe Area
excerpt: BBD preview states that 8,331 persons were killed in the 11-31 July 1995 Srebrenica-fall window.
pages_or_sections: Tom I, Podrinje section, Graph 73 (preview excerpt)
transform_note: Used as the best total direct-death figure for the episode, even though its geographic window is slightly wider than the core 11-19 July chronology.
confidence_note: Moderate; single registry source, but unusually specific.

victim_side: Bosniak soldiers / personnel coded as soldiers in the Bosnian Book of the Dead
geographic_scope: Srebrenica-related municipalities in Podrinje tied to the fall of the UN Safe Area
excerpt: BBD preview gives 1,416 soldiers among the 8,331 deaths linked to the fall of Srebrenica.
pages_or_sections: Tom I, Podrinje section, Graph 73 (preview excerpt)
transform_note: Used as the only located source-grounded soldier death count for this narrow episode; HRDAG warns that the underlying status field likely over-reports soldiers.
confidence_note: Low-to-moderate because the status field is operational and potentially biased.

victim_side: Bosnian Muslim victims of the Srebrenica genocide
excerpt: Remembering Srebrenica provides a named-victim headline count of 8,372.
transform_note: Used only as corroborating high-end total context, not as the core DR numerator because no status split accompanies it.
confidence_note: Useful corroboration, but not directly usable for soldier/civilian splitting.

excerpt: ICTY para. 594 gives 7,000-8,000 massacred men of military age plus 25,000 forcibly transferred survivors.
transform_note: Legal-context total for the massacre process; not used as the core episode total because later registries are slightly higher and more specific.
confidence_note: Useful lower/earlier benchmark rather than the final best total.
